_comment: DEV-ONLY guided placement plan for /cobblemon-initiative dev place (devtest/DevPlaceManager). Proposals only — the showrunner walk (next/here/adopt) produces {world}/data/npc_placements.json, which Claude folds into dialog-src. Strip with the devtest package at 1.0.0 (TODO section 2).

entries.name: Naoko
entries.purpose: Gym-2-era road grunt (gig-contractor, grumbles about pensions); first Company muscle on the Takehara Falls → Hua Zhan City walk, watching the wheat traffic past Firstfurrow Farm.
entries.idealSpot: On the east-west stretch of Harvest Road where Firstfurrow Farm's fenceline runs along the north side — plant her mid-road at the farm gate, facing east so her 8-block pursue cone catches players walking in from Takehara Falls. The farm behind her sells the Company-eyeing-the-wheat beat without any set dressing.

entries.name: Bello
entries.purpose: Gym-3-era grunt ('only contracted through Friday') screening the marsh road between Hua Zhan City and Mystic Marsh; softens the player up before Shade's Regional Office checkpoint.
entries.idealSpot: Mid-corridor on Willowmire Path where the path runs a straight ~20-block-wide ribbon between the willows/marsh edges — stand him center-path facing east toward the Hua Zhan end so westbound players walk straight into the cone. Avoid the water side; keep him on packed path so pursue doesn't strand him in mud.

entries.name: Regional Manager Shade
entries.purpose: Act-1 management boss (doubles, 3000 prize) running the Company 'Regional Office' — half-recognises the amnesiac player; the gate beat right before the player enters Mystic Marsh for gym 3.
entries.idealSpot: At the far west end of Willowmire Path where it spills into Mystic Marsh's eastern outskirts — look for (or earmark) a porch, kiosk, or small structure that can read as a roadside Regional Office desk, and stand Shade in front of it facing east back down the path so nobody enters town without passing the checkpoint. If no structure exists, a spot where the path pinches against terrain works; the key is players funnel past within 12 blocks.

entries.name: Ekon
entries.purpose: Gym-4-era Operations Center grunt logging traveler 'compliance' on the descent into Deepcore City; met walking Quarry Road south from Mystic Marsh.
entries.idealSpot: On the diagonal cut of the southern Quarry Road segment where the road descends toward Deepcore between quarry rock faces — center him in the cut facing northeast up-road, so players coming downhill from Mystic Marsh walk into the cone with walls on both sides making him hard to skirt.

entries.name: Petra
entries.purpose: Gym-5-era compliance grunt ('section nine, subsection you') auditing traffic mid-way along the coast road from Deepcore City to Gaviota Port.
entries.idealSpot: Midway along Gullwing Coast where the road hugs the shoreline — stand her on the road itself (not the beach) facing east toward the Deepcore end so westbound players cross her cone. Pick a point where sea on one side and rising ground on the other pinch the walkable width under ~12 blocks.

entries.name: Senior Director Vex
entries.purpose: Gym-5 management boss (doubles, 4500 prize) at the Company 'Operations Center' — panics because the player's face was 'zeroed out years ago'; fought after Petra, on the final approach into Gaviota Port.
entries.idealSpot: At the bend where Gullwing Coast turns south above Westwind Fields farm — a natural spot for an Operations Center structure with sightlines over both the wheat fields and the harbor approach. Place Vex on the outside of the corner facing east along the incoming road, so players bound for Gaviota round the bend into his cone and can't cut the corner past him.

entries.name: Riku
entries.purpose: Gym-6-era market-analyst grunt ('the CobbleDollar has always been fine') posted alone on the long desert leg toward Kalahar Reach.
entries.idealSpot: On the long north-south stretch of Dunewind Trail, mid-leg where the trail pinches between dunes — center him on the track facing north so players heading south from the Deepcore/Gullwing junction walk into the cone. The emptiness is the point: he should be the only figure visible on the horizon for this lonely stretch.

entries.name: Sade
entries.purpose: Gym-7-era elite checkpoint guard ('you cleared my checkpoint the hard way') on the pylon line climbing from the Kalahar leg up into Cyber City; last road grunt before the HQ raid.
entries.idealSpot: On the larger northern Pylon Path zone (the Kalahar→Cyber leg), mid-path where the diagonal corridor is only ~30 blocks wide — stand her at the base of a pylon facing south-downhill so players climbing toward Cyber City's south edge enter the cone head-on. The pylon at her back frames the checkpoint.

entries.name: Osamu
entries.purpose: HQ-raid outer perimeter guard — the first Company body fought on The Company, Inc. grounds when the raid opens after gym 7.
entries.idealSpot: At the mouth of the southern driveway lobe of the HQ compound, where the grounds funnel in from the Cyber City streets — center him on the entryway facing south so anyone stepping onto Company property is spotted immediately. Keep him outdoors at the gate line, not up against the building.

entries.name: Hui
entries.purpose: HQ-raid inner perimeter guard ('the wall between the public and the truth') — second outdoor fight, between the gate and the HQ front doors.
entries.idealSpot: On the outdoor walkway between the south gate and the HQ entrance, roughly halfway up the courtyard — face south so players who just dropped Osamu at the gate walk straight up into the second cone with no gap between the two fights. Stay outside the doors; the interior belongs to Noir.

entries.name: COO Noir
entries.purpose: The last gate of the HQ raid — disciplined COO who recognised the player from the security feed; beating him opens the door to Acting CEO DJ, the act-2 climax.
entries.idealSpot: Inside the HQ approach at the final corridor or doorway just before DJ's room (DJ anchor is 1590 51 1028) — plant him square in the doorframe, back to the last door, facing the direction the player enters so he physically cannot be walked around. Fine-tune to whatever the interior's actual last chokepoint is.

entries.name: Ivo
entries.purpose: Gym-10-era Boardroom door guard ('they told me nobody gets past me') — the beat that reopens HQ for the Board of Directors clearout after the Royal League.
entries.idealSpot: Back inside HQ near the DJ anchor — locate the Boardroom door on foot and plant Ivo square in front of it, back to the door, facing down the approach hallway so the player must engage before the board. Coordinates are a starting point at the anchor; the real spot is wherever the boardroom entrance actually is.

entries.name: Acting CEO DJ
entries.purpose: Act-2 climax of the HQ raid (after gym 7): the seat-warmer CEO who refuses a meeting until 4 wheat fields are liberated, then battles for the chair; despawns on defeat and triggers hq_stabilize.
entries.idealSpot: Top floor of the HQ tower at the anchor — find the executive office with a desk and a window (his dialog points out the window at the wheat fields). Stand him behind the desk facing the stair/elevator landing so the sight cone catches players as they crest the last flight. y 51 is only the anchor guess; if the executive floor sits higher, walk the interior stairs to the topmost room and place him there instead.

entries.name: L§kaaaaa
entries.purpose: Act 3, The Boardroom (champion-gated): the Communications director who wrote the founder out of the story; one of four board fights that must fall before the Founder takes a meeting.
entries.idealSpot: First chair on the LEFT (west) side of the boardroom table as you enter, nearest the door — facing across the table so she's the first face a player reads on walking in. If the real boardroom is on another floor of the tower, keep the four-around-a-table + head-chair arrangement and shift the whole cluster together.

entries.name: M§kaaaaaaaaa
entries.purpose: Act 3, The Boardroom (champion-gated): the board member who proposed the removal motion; one of the four seats to clear before the Founder.
entries.idealSpot: First chair on the RIGHT (east) side of the table, opposite Lauren, nearest the door — facing the entrance so she and Lauren bracket the player like a receiving line. Interior may differ from this proposal; preserve the two-per-side pairing when relocating.

entries.name: M§kaaa
entries.purpose: Act 3, The Boardroom (champion-gated): the protege who signed the founder's removal; one of the four board fights gating the Founder.
entries.idealSpot: Second chair on the LEFT (west) side, one seat deeper than Lauren toward the head of the table — angled slightly toward the door so his intro fires as the player passes the first pair. Keep a few blocks between all four so the fights read as separate chairs in one room; slide the cluster wholesale if the real boardroom is elsewhere in the tower.

entries.name: M§kaaaa
entries.purpose: Act 3, The Boardroom (champion-gated): the accountant who reserved against the founder's return; last of the four board seats before the Founder, whose clearout unlocks the level-100 cap.
entries.idealSpot: Second chair on the RIGHT (east) side, opposite Matt and one seat from the head chair — facing across the table. He's the deepest board seat, so the player should pass him last before reaching the Founder. Same caveat: if the boardroom is on another floor, move all five stations together and keep this seat adjacent to the head chair.

entries.name: §kfounder
entries.purpose: The true final boss: the player's shadow self at the head of the table, gated on all four board defeats; a level-100 mirror battle whose win fires reveal/founder_defeated and the company_overthrown tag.
entries.idealSpot: The head chair at the FAR (south) end of the table, centered on its long axis, behind all four board seats — facing straight back down the table toward the door so the mirror reveal only lands once the room between is cleared. Leave ~5 blocks between the nearest board pair and this spot. If the real boardroom differs, the one non-negotiable is: Founder at the head, the four board members between the entrance and the chair.

entries.name: High Priestess Aurora
entries.purpose: Final battle of the Fairy Shrine gauntlet (opens after Mystic Marsh, gym 3; team tops at 48 with Xerneas). Defeating her grants fairy_shrine_complete and the fairy shrine crystal.
entries.idealSpot: The anchor is at y=-7, so this chamber is deep underground — /tp will land you in the shrine's inner sanctum. Stand her at the shrine centerpiece at the east end of the room, about 4 blocks east of the last cultist pair, facing west down the cultist gauntlet line so her sight cone catches players finishing the fourth cultist. If there is a glowing crystal or light feature, put her directly in front of it so she silhouettes against it.

entries.name: High Priest Terran
entries.purpose: Final battle of the Ground Shrine gauntlet (opens after Kalahar Reach, gym 6; team tops at 60 with Landorus). Defeating him grants ground_shrine_complete and the ground shrine crystal.
entries.idealSpot: At the shrine centerpiece on the elevated floor (y=83), east end of the cultist line, facing west toward the approach so players walk the four-cultist gauntlet straight into him. He kneels-and-keeps-vigil in dialog — prefer a flat stone floor spot at the altar rather than a ledge, with clear line of sight down the entry path.

entries.name: High Priest Draconis
entries.purpose: Final battle of the Dragon Shrine gauntlet (opens after Ryujin Keep, gym 8; team tops at 75 with Rayquaza). Defeating him grants dragon_shrine_complete and the dragon shrine crystal.
entries.idealSpot: At the shrine centerpiece, easternmost point of the trainer line, facing west over the two cultist pairs so he confronts players as they clear the gauntlet. His dialog is about a coiled ancient fire — if the shrine has a brazier, dragon statue, or fire feature, stand him at its base with the feature behind him and the approach corridor in front.

entries.name: High Priest Glacius
entries.purpose: Final battle of the Ice Shrine gauntlet (opens after Nifl Town, gym 9; team tops at 78 with Kyurem). Defeating him grants ice_shrine_complete and the ice shrine crystal.
entries.idealSpot: At the shrine centerpiece at the east end of the cultist line, facing west toward the approach. Note: this anchor sits ~33 blocks outside the install.json zone polygon (nearest edge runs (3670,1981)→(3685,1952)); the entire five-trainer ice line (x=3634–3644) is west of the polygon, so the polygon is misdrawn — trust the anchor, which matches the authored trainer coordinate exactly. His theme is stillness and reflection: prefer a spot on packed ice or before an ice-mirror feature where he can stand perfectly centered and motionless as the player walks in.

entries.name: High Priest Ignis
entries.purpose: Final battle of the Fire Shrine gauntlet — the endgame shrine, gated on royal_champion (team tops at 82 with Heatran). Defeating him grants fire_shrine_complete and the fire shrine crystal (plus a Master Ball and netherite).
entries.idealSpot: The anchor y=51 is well below the zone's surface, so expect a sunken caldera or interior chamber — /tp and orient from inside. Stand him at the flame centerpiece at the east end of the cultist line, facing west down the gauntlet. His dialog says he feeds the flame: place him within arm's reach of the fire feature (brazier, lava basin) so it reads as tending it, sight cone covering the entry.

entries.name: Elite Four Aria
entries.purpose: First of the Elite Four (gated on scorchspire_leader, gym 10); Ghost/Psychic, team tops at 74. She is the League's opening test of nerve — the player meets her immediately after entering the League building.
entries.idealSpot: The five League trainers form a west-to-east spine at z=2773, y=166; Aria is the westernmost, so she takes the FIRST chamber off the entrance hall. Her gimmick is battling in the dark — pick the first enclosed room along the progression route, kill or dim the light sources, and stand her at the far (east) end facing the entry door so players step out of the bright hall into her shadowed room. If the authored 4-block spacing lands mid-hall, push her west to the first proper chamber threshold.

entries.name: Elite Four Marcus
entries.purpose: Second of the Elite Four (unlocks after Aria); Rock, team tops at 76 with Rhyperior. The endurance wall of the gauntlet.
entries.idealSpot: Second chamber on the west-to-east spine, one room east of Aria's. He is 'all stone' — favor a chamber with exposed rock/stone brick, or dress one with boulders. Plant him dead center of the room like a standing monolith, facing west at the doorway from Aria's chamber so he is the first thing seen on entry. If the building is one long hall, space him a full room-width (~12+ blocks) east of Aria rather than the authored 4.

entries.name: Elite Four Luna
entries.purpose: Third of the Elite Four (unlocks after Marcus); Dark, team tops at 78 with Hydreigon. She reads the player and half-recognizes the redacted founder.
entries.idealSpot: Third chamber on the spine, east of Marcus. Her hall is described as colder and gloomier than his — low light but not Aria's blackout: soul lanterns or sparse candles work. Place her off-center, near a wall or pillar in partial shadow, facing west toward the door so she appears half-hidden until the player is well inside the room.

entries.name: Elite Four Drake
entries.purpose: Fourth and last of the Elite Four (unlocks after Luna); Dragon, team tops at 80 with Haxorus. The final wall before the throne.
entries.idealSpot: Fourth chamber, last room before the champion's hall. He calls himself the final wall — stand him squarely in the middle of the doorway axis to Cynthia's throne room, facing west, physically blocking the line the player walks. If the chamber has dragon statues or a raised battle floor, center him between them with the throne-room doors at his back.

entries.name: Champion Cynthia
entries.purpose: Champion of the Royal League (unlocks after Drake); team tops at 85 with Garchomp. Defeating her grants royal_league_champion, unlocks level cap 85, and pivots the story into act 3.
entries.idealSpot: Easternmost point of the spine — the throne room / summit hall at the end of the gauntlet. Her dialog is all about watching the challenger arrive ('I knew you the moment you stepped into the light'): put her on the highest floor feature available — dais, throne steps, or summit platform — centered, facing west down the full length of the hall so the player sees her from the moment Drake's doors open. Leave enough flat floor in front of her for the battle camera.

entries.name: Warehouse Barrel (verify only — DO NOT SPAWN)
entries.purpose: The Incomplete File stage 1 ledger pickup — SUPERSEDED as an NPC: it is now a Java click-intercept (DocPropManager) on the real barrel block; spawning this preset would stack a duplicate clickable on the block.
entries.idealSpot: Verification stop only: confirm a real barrel block sits at 2584 107 2925 by the Sango warehouse and that clicking it fires give_doc_ledger instead of opening. Place nothing here.

entries.name: Dusty Crate (verify only — DO NOT SPAWN)
entries.purpose: The Incomplete File stage 1 portrait pickup — SUPERSEDED as an NPC: it is a Java click-intercept on the real chest block (registered before LootChest); spawning this preset would duplicate the clickable.
entries.idealSpot: Verification stop only: confirm the real chest at 2591 111 2815 (about 10 blocks from Lumo's courier cart, near the liaison fence spot at 2581,111,2822) fires give_doc_portrait on click and is not loot-stocked. Place nothing here.

entries.name: Petal Courier Mio
entries.purpose: Runs the Petal Sprint — a 120s timed delivery from the Sango mouth of Blossom Path to the delivery bell at the Takehara arch; act-1 side content with a daily rematch.
entries.idealSpot: Sango-side mouth of Blossom Path, standing on a painted start line facing west up the route she times. Stationary look-only so the line never moves; the bell prop must land inside the finish box (x1918 dx12 / z2578 dz12 at the 1923,2584 arch), and while here repoint the sprint fail-teleport (provisional 2505 71 2850 in sprint/fail.mcfunction) to a fall-safe, suffocation-safe spot beside her final position — hardcore run.

entries.name: Femi (KYC Agent)
entries.purpose: Door-to-door Company account-verification canvasser; opt-in battle (Sentret 14/Meowth 15) gated on defeated_takehara_leader — tolerably the player's first open Company fight; despawns on defeat so the clipboard man stops coming around Sango.
entries.idealSpot: Sango approach end of Blossom Path, behind a small survey table with a clipboard (table is set dressing), facing east toward Sango so backtracking players walk straight into the desk. No sight registration — he is interaction-driven, never an ambusher.

entries.name: Revision Notice 1
entries.purpose: Incomplete File stage 2 pull-marker 1 of 3 — a freshly pasted Revision Notice on Binta's patrol loop; pull button lights once Lucian stage 1 is filed (docs_filed).
entries.idealSpot: A wall, fence, or signpost on the Sango-side stretch of Blossom Path inside the surveyor's loop; disguise via Easy NPC model settings (tiny scale or invisible skin flat against a real poster) — the click is the point, not the body. Face it toward the road.

entries.name: Forewoman Tetsu
entries.purpose: Work Orders quest hub — three contracts posted at her waystation board (functions under sidequest/work_orders/*); met mid-Blossom-Path during the gym-1 stretch.
entries.idealSpot: The mid-route waystation shack (shack + posted work-order board are set dressing via snbt_merge.py); stand her beside the board facing the road so passers-by read board-then-forewoman. Stationary look-only, no sight registration — she must always be at her board.

entries.name: Apiarist Sumi
entries.purpose: Work Orders fork B — the honey fetch beside her emptied apiary; turn-in gifts a female Combee lv8 (Vespiquen line, mirroring Leader Cicada's ace; givemon gender key needs its smoke test, fallback is a sanctioned spawn+ball catch).
entries.idealSpot: Her plot in the meadow just off the waystation, standing beside the emptied apiary; dress the plot with grey survey stakes and string a few wild bee hives along the route east of her toward Sango as the honey source. Stationary look-only, facing the path.

entries.name: Binta (Company Surveyor)
entries.purpose: Incomplete File stage 2 (badges 3+) — walks a fixed pasting loop past all three Revision Notices; when her sightline lands on a player holding pulled notices the tick function fires LOGGED and zeroes the count. Never battles, never damages.
entries.idealSpot: Patrol anchor on the path spine between the waystation and the checkpoint; from here set her Easy NPC patrol waypoints to sweep past posts 1 and 2 on Blossom Path and out through Takehara to post 3 on Harvest Road. After import: tag <entity> add surveyor, then npcsight-register her uuid — the @e[tag=surveyor] selector and her sight cone are the quest's stealth mechanic.

entries.name: Field Researcher Ume
entries.purpose: Head Count census quest-giver (honor-system capture self-report) plus an optional wager battle (sq_headcount_wager lv12, purse 900); Company-CONTRACTED cheerful HR-speak, zero recognition.
entries.idealSpot: Mid-meadow among the flowers at a folding table with clipboard-and-instruments set dressing, table facing the road so approaching players see the desk before her. Stationary look-only, no sight registration.

entries.name: Revision Notice 2
entries.purpose: Incomplete File stage 2 pull-marker 2 of 3, farther up Binta's loop toward the checkpoint.
entries.idealSpot: Same disguised-prop pattern as post 1, but pick a surface where pulling it means standing in the open — exposed ground about 45 blocks downhill of the checkpoint tent, no cover between the notice and the path, so the pull risks Binta's sight cone.

entries.name: Sani (Checkpoint Agent)
entries.purpose: Per My Last Memo — left flank of the Voluntary Verification Checkpoint at the Blossom pinch; carries the eavesdrop (8 unseen seconds at the tent sets memo_loiter, arming the ON_DISTANCE_NEAR overheard_memo chain) and the contraband/dead-letter entry.
entries.idealSpot: Left flank of the pop-up checkpoint tent where the path pinches, facing down-path toward Sango so his hail (range 12, mode dialog — never pursue) catches players walking up. Ensure cover exists within earshot (behind the tent or a rock) where a player can stand unseen for the eavesdrop; after import: tag checkpoint_agent, npcsight add <uuid> 12 checkpoint_hail, npcsight mode <uuid> dialog.

entries.name: Haruki (Checkpoint Agent)
entries.purpose: Per My Last Memo — right flank of the checkpoint tent; shares the fee and contraband tags with Sani (paying once covers the checkpoint); opt-in battle, prize 240.
entries.idealSpot: Right flank of the same tent, a few blocks from Sani, angled so their two sight cones overlap across the full width of the pinch and nobody can slip the checkpoint unhailed. Same post-import steps: tag checkpoint_agent, npcsight add <uuid> 12 checkpoint_hail, npcsight mode <uuid> dialog.

entries.name: Revision Notice 3
entries.purpose: Incomplete File stage 2 pull-marker 3 of 3 — the Harvest Road end of the surveyor loop; the far pull that forces the player to cross Binta's sight line twice.
entries.idealSpot: On the east-west Harvest Road stretch between Takehara and Hua Zhan (Luo Shiming's trainer body stands ~100 blocks west at y88): paste it on a fence or wall on the north verge facing the road, positioned so the only walk back to the other posts is along the road Binta patrols.

entries.name: Wen (Greenhouse Docent)
entries.purpose: Verified Growth tour docent — propaganda-register floor-by-floor spiel, tags hz_tour_started and stamps completion (greenhouse_kit) behind the wheat_named gate; met in the greenhouse lobby during the Hua Zhan / gym-2 beat.
entries.idealSpot: Greenhouse lobby ground floor at the base of the glass tower, offset a couple of blocks from Kaito Zhang's reserved body at (1542,88,2110) to avoid stacking (Narin Chen holds the mezzanine above); face him at the lobby entrance so arriving players meet the tour pitch first. Stationary look-only.

entries.name: Field Guard — Mirebloom Paddies
entries.purpose: field guard — beating them fires liberation/free_field farm_2 (HQ raid gate is 6 of 10 fields per 2026-07-06 ruling; shipped render.mcfunction still says 4)
entries.idealSpot: Stand at the north-west entrance of the paddies where the track from Mystic Marsh (~390 blocks NW) comes in. Look for the gap in the paddy dikes or the fence opening on that side and face outward up the road so the sight cone covers players walking down from Mystic Marsh.

entries.name: Field Guard — Westwind Fields
entries.purpose: field guard — beating them fires liberation/free_field farm_3 (HQ raid gate is 6 of 10 fields per 2026-07-06 ruling; shipped render.mcfunction still says 4)
entries.idealSpot: The zone is L-shaped; this spot is in the southern leg, the arm pointing at Gaviota Port (~260 blocks S). Find the gate or fence break where the coastal road enters the fields and face south along it so approaching players from Gaviota walk straight into the sight cone.

entries.name: Field Guard — Dryrow Steading
entries.purpose: field guard — beating them fires liberation/free_field farm_4 (HQ raid gate is 6 of 10 fields per 2026-07-06 ruling; shipped render.mcfunction still says 4)
entries.idealSpot: South-east corner of the steading, on the side facing Kalahar Reach (~550 blocks ESE). Put the guard at the main gate or the break in the dry-field fencing there, facing east down the approach road; keep buildings behind them so the sightline is clean.

entries.name: Field Guard — Crossroads Granary
entries.purpose: field guard — beating them fires liberation/free_field farm_5 (HQ raid gate is 6 of 10 fields per 2026-07-06 ruling; shipped render.mcfunction still says 4)
entries.idealSpot: North edge of the granary compound, where the road from Sango Town (~700 blocks N) meets the property — this farm sits on the Sango–Kalahar crossroads, so pick the junction-side gate. Face north up the road; players coming from Sango should hit the sight cone before reaching the granary doors.

entries.name: Field Guard — Fenceline Acres
entries.purpose: field guard — beating them fires liberation/free_field farm_6 (HQ raid gate is 6 of 10 fields per 2026-07-06 ruling; shipped render.mcfunction still says 4)
entries.idealSpot: Southern fence line, at the gate facing Hua Zhan City (~360 blocks S). The farm is named for its fencing, so find the actual gate post in the south fence and plant the guard just inside it, facing south down the road toward Hua Zhan.

entries.name: Field Guard — Coldfurrow Farm
entries.purpose: field guard — beating them fires liberation/free_field farm_7 (HQ raid gate is 6 of 10 fields per 2026-07-06 ruling; shipped render.mcfunction still says 4)
entries.idealSpot: Mid-field on the eastern approach — Ryujin Keep is ~320 blocks due east and Cyber City is west, so traffic crosses this farm east-west. Find the field entrance or path gap on the east side and face east toward Ryujin so players walking the keep road get spotted at the gate.

entries.name: Field Guard — Frostfallow Farm
entries.purpose: field guard — beating them fires liberation/free_field farm_8 (HQ raid gate is 6 of 10 fields per 2026-07-06 ruling; shipped render.mcfunction still says 4)
entries.idealSpot: The zone is a thin NE–SW diagonal strip; this spot is on its spine. Sango Town is SW and Nifl/Royal League are NE-SE, so place the guard at whichever end of the strip the road actually crosses — default to the south-west entrance facing Sango so gym-route players walking up from town trip the sight cone.

entries.name: Field Guard — Highfield Estate
entries.purpose: field guard — beating them fires liberation/free_field farm_9 (HQ raid gate is 6 of 10 fields per 2026-07-06 ruling; shipped render.mcfunction still says 4)
entries.idealSpot: North side of the estate, at the gate facing the Royal League road (~580 blocks N). An estate should have a formal entrance — use the driveway or gatehouse if one exists and face north along the approach; players heading to or from the League pass this way.

entries.name: Field Guard — Ashloam Fields
entries.purpose: field guard — beating them fires liberation/free_field farm_10 (HQ raid gate is 6 of 10 fields per 2026-07-06 ruling; shipped render.mcfunction still says 4)
entries.idealSpot: South-east corner of the fields, on the side facing the Scorchspire road (~650 blocks SE). Find the field entrance or fence break nearest that road and face south-east along it so players on the volcano approach are seen at the boundary.
